name: Kabyle

allophone_rules.notes: Spirantization of /b/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /m/ (e.g., zembil 'basket' вЖТ [z…Щmbil], not *[z…Щmќ≤il]). Kossmann & Stroomer (1997) p.469 (Riffian illustration, extended to Kabyle by analogy). Corroborated for Chemini Kabyle by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022): /nќ≤/ вЖТ [mb] and /mќ≤/ вЖТ [mb]; this occlusivization is constant across Chemini, A√ѓt Mengellat, Boghni, and Makouda.

Spirantization of /d/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /n/ (e.g., anda 'where' вЖТ [anda], not *[an√∞a]). Kossmann & Stroomer (1997) p.469 (Riffian example, extended to Kabyle by analogy). Corroborated for Chemini Kabyle by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022): /n√∞/ вЖТ [nd].

Spirantization of /t/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /n/ (e.g., anta 'which one (f.)' вЖТ [anta], not *[anќЄa]; also corpus-attested: ante…£). Anta is native Kabyle, feminine of anwa 'which one (m.)'. Kossmann & Stroomer (1997) p.469 (Riffian example, extended to Kabyle by analogy). Corroborated for Chemini Kabyle by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022): /nќЄ/ вЖТ [nt].

Spirantization of word-final /t/ is blocked after /l/ (e.g., tamellalt 'egg' вЖТ [ќЄam…Щllalt], not *[ќЄam…ЩllaќЄ]). Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022) attest /lќЄ,l√∞/ вЖТ [lt,ld] as a general cluster rule in Chemini Kabyle, not restricted to word-final position; this spec keeps the rule word-final-only pending confirmation that the same blocking holds word-medially.

Spirantization of /dЋ§/ is blocked after a homorganic nasal /n/. Based on Kossmann & Stroomer (1997)/analogy and native-speaker judgment; not covered by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022), whose study treats only the non-emphatic non-strident fricatives /ќ≤, ќЄ, √∞, √І,  Э/.
Spirantization of /d/ is blocked after /l/ (e.g., Ldi 'open' вЖТ [ldi], not [l√∞i]).

Spirantization of /t/ is blocked after /m/ (e.g., tasemmumt вЖТ [tasemmumt], not [tasemmumќЄ]). Attested for Chemini and A√ѓt Mengellat Kabyle by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022): /mќЄ/ вЖТ [mt]. Boghni and Makouda do not show this occlusivization (spirantization survives: [mќЄ]); this spec follows the Chemini/A√ѓt Mengellat pattern.

Spirantization of /d/ is blocked after /m/ (e.g., ќЄamda 'pond' вЖТ [ќЄamda], not [ќЄam√∞a]). Corroborated for Chemini and A√ѓt Mengellat Kabyle by Bedar, Quellec & Tifrit (2022): /m√∞/ вЖТ [md]. Boghni and Makouda do not show this occlusivization (spirantization survives: [m√∞]); this spec follows the Chemini/A√ѓt Mengellat pattern.
Spirantization of /k Ј/ is blocked after any consonant: /k Ј/ вЖТ [√І Ј] only post-vocalically or word-initially.
Spirantization of /k/ is blocked after any consonant, including geminates: /k/ вЖТ [√І] only post-vocalically or word-initially.
Spirantization of /…° Ј/ is blocked after any consonant EXCEPT /m/: /…° Ј/ вЖТ [ Э Ј] post-vocalically, word-initially, and after /m/.
Spirantization of /…°/ is blocked after any consonant EXCEPT /m/: /…°/ вЖТ [ Э] post-vocalically, word-initially, and after /m/.
Spirantization of the lax labiovelar stop: /k Ј/ вЖТ [√І Ј] (post-vocalically and word-initially; blocked post-consonantally). Geminate вЯ®kkwвЯ© = /kЋР Ј/ stays a stop.
Spirantization of the lax velar stop, with palatal shift: /k/ вЖТ [√І]. Geminate вЯ®kkвЯ© = /kЋР/ stays a stop.
Spirantization of the lax voiced labiovelar stop: /…° Ј/ вЖТ [ Э Ј] (post-vocalically and word-initially; blocked post-consonantally except after /m/). Geminate вЯ®ggwвЯ© = /…°ЋР Ј/ stays a stop.
Spirantization of the lax (non-geminate) bilabial stop: /b/ вЖТ [ќ≤].
Spirantization of the lax dental stop: /d/ вЖТ [√∞].
Spirantization of the lax voiceless dental stop: /t/ вЖТ [ќЄ].
Spirantization of the lax velar stop: /…°/ вЖТ [ Э].
Spirantization of the lax emphatic dental stop: /dЋ§/ вЖТ [√∞Ћ§].
/…£ Ј/ is realized as the labialized uvular fricative [ Б Ј] in all positions.
Geminate вЯ®…£…£wвЯ© = /…£ЋР Ј/ is realized as long labialized uvular [ БЋР Ј].
/…£/ is realized as the uvular fricative [ Б] in all positions.
Geminate вЯ®…£…£вЯ© = /…£ЋР/ is realized as long uvular [ БЋР].
Vowel backing: /a/ вЖТ […С] before emphatic, uvular, and labio-uvular consonants.
Vowel backing: /a/ вЖТ […С] after emphatic, uvular, and labio-uvular consonants.
Default realization of /a/ as [√¶] when not backed by emphatic/uvular context.
Vowel lowering: /i/ вЖТ […™] in closed syllables (before consonant clusters).
Vowel lowering: /u/ вЖТ [ К] in closed syllables (before consonant clusters).
Nasal place assimilation: /n/ вЖТ [≈Л] before a dorsal (velar/uvular/labiovelar) consonant.
Nasal place assimilation: /n/ вЖТ [m] before a labial consonant.
Nasal place assimilation: /n/ вЖТ […≤] before a palatal consonant /j/.

notes: Kabyle (Taqbaylit), Northern Berber (Amazigh) language of Kabylia, north-eastern Algeria, ~5-7 million speakers (Kossmann & Stroomer 1997, Berber Phonology, p.461). INPUT CONTRACT: this spec expects text in the standardised Berber LATIN alphabet (the tamazi…£t/INALCO orthography descending from Mammeri's conventions, used by the Kabyle Wikipedia, the press, and Na√ѓt-Zerrad's teaching grammars). Tifinagh (the neo-Tifinagh abjad-alphabet) is a secondary script for Kabyle and is not modelled here; a Tifinagh text must be transliterated to the Latin orthography first. Hallmark grapheme->IPA correspondences: c=/ Г/, ƒН=/tЌ° Г/, «І=/dЌ° Т/, j=/ Т/, …£=/…£/, deterministically realised uvular [ Б] (the Kabyle norm, all positions; broad […£] documented as variant), x=/ѕЗ/, бЄ•=/ƒІ/, …Ы=/ Х/, q=/q/, and the emphatic (pharyngealized) series бєЫ бє£ бЄН бє≠ бЇУ = /rЋ§ sЋ§ dЋ§ tЋ§ zЋ§/. Labiovelarized consonants (written via trigraphes/digraphes kkw, kw, ggw, gw, qqw, qw, xxw, xw, …£…£w, …£w) are fully modeled with their respective labialized IPA equivalents using the U+02B7 modifier letter  Ј.

ancestors.notes: Immediate Glottolog subgroup (kaby1244): Afro-Asiatic > Berber > Kabyle-Atlas Berber > Kabyle. Traditionally within Northern Berber.

Arabic adstrate/superstrate: heavy lexical borrowing and the source of most of Kabyle's non-Proto-Berber emphatics and pharyngeals (бЄ• /ƒІ/, …Ы / Х/, бє£ /sЋ§/, бє≠ /tЋ§/), per Kossmann & Stroomer 1997 p.464.
